verses.text: The ark rests at Kirjath-jearim for twenty long years, and at last 'all the house of Israel lamented after the LORD.' Sometimes God lets a barren season run on until His people feel their need. A holy homesickness for God is itself the first stirring of revival.

The ark rests at Kirjath-jearim for twenty long years, and at last 'all the house of Israel lamented after the LORD.' Sometimes God lets a barren season run on until His people feel their need. A holy homesickness for God is itself the first stirring of revival.

Samuel calls them to prove their longing real: 'put away the strange gods... and prepare your hearts unto the LORD, and serve him only.' And they do. Repentance is not mere regret but the turning of the whole heart from idols to serve God alone.

Samuel calls them to prove their longing real: 'put away the strange gods... and prepare your hearts unto the LORD, and serve him only.' And they do. Repentance is not mere regret but the turning of the whole heart from idols to serve God alone.

At Mizpah they gather, pour out water before the LORD, fast, and confess, 'We have sinned.' True repentance is humble and open-handed, holding nothing back. A people poured out before God in honest confession is exactly the people ready for His help.

At Mizpah they gather, pour out water before the LORD, fast, and confess, 'We have sinned.' True repentance is humble and open-handed, holding nothing back. A people poured out before God in honest confession is exactly the people ready for His help.

The Philistines attack the moment Israel turns to God — the enemy always opposes repentance. But Samuel offers a lamb and cries to the LORD, and God hears him. When we meet the foe at our weakest, a mediator's prayer and a sacrifice are our refuge.

The Philistines attack the moment Israel turns to God — the enemy always opposes repentance. But Samuel offers a lamb and cries to the LORD, and God hears him. When we meet the foe at our weakest, a mediator's prayer and a sacrifice are our refuge.

The Philistines attack the moment Israel turns to God — the enemy always opposes repentance. But Samuel offers a lamb and cries to the LORD, and God hears him. When we meet the foe at our weakest, a mediator's prayer and a sacrifice are our refuge.

As Samuel offers, the LORD thunders from heaven and throws the Philistines into panic, and Israel routs them. The God they sought now fights for them. The battle they could never have won, He wins; deliverance comes down from heaven, not up from their own strength.

As Samuel offers, the LORD thunders from heaven and throws the Philistines into panic, and Israel routs them. The God they sought now fights for them. The battle they could never have won, He wins; deliverance comes down from heaven, not up from their own strength.

Samuel sets up a stone, Ebenezer — 'hitherto hath the LORD helped us' — and the Philistines trouble Israel no more in his days. It is good to mark God's mercies with a memorial. Remembering past help is the surest fuel for present faith.

Samuel sets up a stone, Ebenezer — 'hitherto hath the LORD helped us' — and the Philistines trouble Israel no more in his days. It is good to mark God's mercies with a memorial. Remembering past help is the surest fuel for present faith.

Samuel sets up a stone, Ebenezer — 'hitherto hath the LORD helped us' — and the Philistines trouble Israel no more in his days. It is good to mark God's mercies with a memorial. Remembering past help is the surest fuel for present faith.

Samuel judges Israel all his days, going on circuit year by year, and builds an altar at his home in Ramah. A faithful leader serves steadily, brings justice to the people, and keeps worship at the centre of his own household. Faithfulness is a long obedience.
